component.name: VOCO
component.summary: A voice-native interface layer designed for speed and precision

description.p: VOCO is a Linux desktop dictation tool for people who want fast, private voice capture with local transcription by default.
It stays in the tray, listens on demand, and keeps visible state compact. Its optional realtime conversation mode sends microphone audio to OpenAI only after the user explicitly starts that mode.

ul.li: Tray-first workflow with a compact command panel
Onboarding for microphone setup and hotkeys
GitHub Release update checks for manual install channels
Clear Linux-specific install, upgrade, and troubleshooting guidance

description.p: Adds persistent VOCO Dictation input-source support with revisable live preedit, authoritative local checkpoints, exact-prefix delivery, and recoverable fail-closed final transcripts.
Unifies tray and settings state, adds safe configuration recovery and single-instance ownership, and hardens model-cache integrity, installers, private IPC, and release verification.
Adds bounded 10-minute dictation, chunked local transcription, append-only live cursor previews, and safer final transcript reconciliation.
Improves audio capture throughput and memory use, adds optional local model and realtime assistant paths, and hardens Linux packaging and release verification.
